extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property is VIOLATED. hemoptysis appears only in the interaction_drop rule [priority 100]: IF (rash and hemoptysis) THEN acuity := self_care. Because this rule has the highest priority (100, vs 2 for all raise_* rules), it overrides everything when active.

Consider the baseline with rash=true, hemoptysis=FALSE, palpitations=false, vision_loss=false: only raise_rash (priority 2) fires, yielding acuity = urgent.

Now set hemoptysis=TRUE with all else unchanged (rash=true, palpitations=false, vision_loss=false): raise_rash still matches (urgent, priority 2), but interaction_drop now also matches (rash and hemoptysis) and wins with priority 100, forcing acuity := self_care.

Enum order low-to-high is self_care < routine < urgent < emergent. Setting hemoptysis true lowered acuity from urgent to self_care, which is strictly lower — a violation of the requirement that hemoptysis true never lowers acuity.

The witness_assignment given is the post-flip ("hemoptysis set true") input producing self_care; the corresponding pre-flip input is identical except hemoptysis=false, producing urgent.
